name: reviewdog
description: Automated code review and security linting integration for CI/CD pipelines using reviewdog. Aggregates findings from multiple security and quality tools (SAST, linters, formatters) into unified code review comments on pull requests. Use when: (1) Integrating security scanning into code review workflows, (2) Automating security feedback on pull requests, (3) Consolidating multiple tool outputs into actionable review comments, (4) Enforcing secure coding standards in CI/CD pipelines, (5) Providing inline security annotations during development.
capabilities: Integrate 10+ security scanners (Semgrep, Bandit, Gitleaks, Checkov, Hadolint)
Post inline security findings as GitHub/GitLab PR comments
Filter findings to show only new issues in code changes
Configure severity-based gating to block PRs with critical findings
Generate CI/CD configurations for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Map security findings to OWASP Top 10 and CWE categories
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing CI/CD templates and reference materials for reviewdog security integration. All static findings are false positives from legitimate DevSecOps documentation. The skill describes running security scanners (Semgrep, Bandit, Gitleaks) and posting results to PRs - this is standard, documented CI/CD behavior using properly secured token management via GitHub/GitLab secrets.
valueStatement: Security scanning results are scattered across CI logs and missed by reviewers. Reviewdog aggregates findings from multiple security tools into inline pull request comments, making vulnerabilities visible where developers work.
userTitle: Add automated security code review to CI/CD pipelines
limitations: Requires API tokens for PR comment functionality (local reporter works without tokens)
Reviewdog must run in CI environment or local repository context
Does not fix vulnerabilities automatically, only reports findings
Depends on external security tools being installed and configured

useCases.title: Enforce security standards in PRs
useCases.description: Configure reviewdog to block merges when critical vulnerabilities are detected, ensuring security gates are never bypassed.

useCases.title: Automate security feedback loops
useCases.description: Add security scanning to CI pipelines that posts findings directly to pull requests, reducing time to remediation.

useCases.title: Catch issues before merge
useCases.description: Get inline security annotations on vulnerable code patterns during code review, with links to CWE references.

promptTemplates.title: Basic reviewdog setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Add security scanning to a GitHub Actions workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up reviewdog in my GitHub Actions workflow to run Semgrep with error-level findings posted as PR review comments

promptTemplates.title: Multi-tool scanning
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a reviewdog configuration that runs Bandit for Python, Gitleaks for secrets, and Checkov for IaC security in parallel

promptTemplates.title: Severity filtering
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure reviewdog to only show new security issues introduced in the PR and fail the build for critical findings

promptTemplates.title: Custom security rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a custom grep-based security check to my reviewdog configuration that detects dangerous function usage like eval() and exec()

bestPractices: Use fail-on-error=true for critical severity findings to block vulnerable code from merging
Configure filter-mode=added or diff_context to reduce noise from existing issues
Store API tokens in CI secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, GITLAB_TOKEN) rather than hardcoding
antiPatterns: Running reviewdog without filter-mode on large repositories creates overwhelming comment volume
Using personal access tokens instead of CI service tokens with excessive permissions
Skipping baseline files for existing vulnerabilities creates noise when starting adoption

faq.question: What CI platforms does reviewdog support?
faq.answer: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, Jenkins, Azure Pipelines, Bitbucket Pipelines, and Gerrit. Each has specific reporter formats.

faq.question: Which security tools integrate with reviewdog?
faq.answer: Semgrep, Bandit, Gitleaks, Checkov, Hadolint, Trivy, ShellCheck, ESLint, tfsec, and 30+ additional linters and scanners.

faq.question: Does reviewdog require network access?
faq.answer: Only when posting comments to PRs via github-pr-review or similar reporters. Local reporter works offline.

faq.question: How is sensitive data handled?
faq.answer: Security tool output (including potential secrets) is piped to reviewdog and posted as PR comments. Use --no-git flag for gitleaks to avoid scanning git history.

faq.question: Why are no comments appearing on my PR?
faq.answer: Common causes: missing API token permissions, running on main branch instead of PR, filter-mode too restrictive, or reviewdog not in PR context.

faq.question: How is this different from running security tools directly?
faq.answer: Reviewdog aggregates results from multiple tools into unified inline comments, provides diff-aware filtering, and integrates with PR workflows natively.
